shortDescription: German Embassy · Family reunion · Spouse of German/resident · Language A1

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current national visa fee (€75 approx) and German language A1 requirement for spouses on auswaertiges-amt.de before quoting.

alert.title: Sponsor status in Germany
alert.body: Spouse must be German citizen or holder of valid residence permit with sufficient space and income. Verify sponsor status before fee collection.

about.label: Description
about.value: National visa for spouse reunion with German citizen or foreign national holding qualifying residence permit in Germany. Leads to residence permit after entry.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Valid marriage · Sponsor with adequate housing and income · Basic German A1 (generally) · Genuine relationship · Health insurance

about.label: Language requirement
about.value: Spouses generally need German A1 certificate unless exempt (e.g., certain qualifications or EU nationals). Verify exemptions.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: German Embassy in India · Ausländerbehörde in Germany after arrival

about.label: After approval
about.value: Enter Germany; register address; obtain residence permit at local Ausländerbehörde.

eligibility.criterion: Legally valid marriage recognised in Germany
Sponsor is German citizen or qualifying resident

redFlagsBanner: If refused, address embassy concerns on relationship or sponsor eligibility before reapplication.

redFlags.title: Sponsor income/housing insufficient
redFlags.description: Sponsor cannot prove adequate apartment size or income.
redFlags.fix: Collect sponsor employment contract, salary slips, lease with square metres

redFlags.title: Missing A1 German certificate
redFlags.description: Spouse applied without required basic German.
redFlags.fix: Complete Goethe A1 or verify exemption applies

redFlags.title: Weak relationship evidence
redFlags.description: Few photos, no visit history, recent marriage unexplained.
redFlags.fix: Chronological relationship timeline; visit stamps; communication logs

redFlags.title: Marriage not registered/apostilled
redFlags.description: Indian marriage certificate not properly legalised.

redFlags.title: Sponsor permit expiring
redFlags.description: Sponsor residence permit near expiry or uncertain status.
redFlags.fix: Verify sponsor valid long-term status before apply

redFlags.title: Prior refusals hidden
redFlags.description: Schengen or national visa refusals not disclosed.

faqs.q: Can we guarantee spouse visa approval?
faqs.a: No. Embassy assesses marriage genuineness, sponsor status, and requirements. Never promise approval.

faqs.q: Is A1 German mandatory for all spouses?
faqs.a: Generally yes for spouses of non-EU residents; exemptions exist—verify on embassy website for specific case.

faqs.q: How long sponsor must be in Germany?
faqs.a: Depends on sponsor status—German citizens differ from permit holders. Verify current AufenthG requirements.
Family reunion typically requires marriage; registered partnership rules vary—assess separately.
Passport/ID, registration, lease, employment, salary, marriage cert from Standesamt if married in Germany.
Typically 8–16 weeks after complete submission—varies by embassy workload.
Work authorisation depends on residence permit granted—verify at Ausländerbehörde.

compliance: Client service agreement on file before submission
Marriage documents must be genuine and properly legalised
proTips: Verify sponsor status and housing before India-side fee collection
Start A1 German early—exam slots fill fast
Apostille marriage certificate with certified translation
Build relationship timeline with visit evidence
Coordinate with sponsor for original German-side documents
postApproval: Guide on Ausländerbehörde residence permit appointment
Advise on integration courses if required

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Initial Germany spouse reunion visa counselor content.

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Coordinate with sponsor in Germany early—lease and salary docs often delay files.

resources.title: Make it in Germany — Family reunion

dos: Verify sponsor status and housing first
donts: Do not apply without sponsor solvency proof
Do not skip A1 when required
Do not hide prior visa refusals
mistakes: Sponsor lease shows insufficient apartment size
A1 certificate expired or wrong level

sampleDocs.title: Sample passport bio page (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Show clients which pages to scan — MRZ visible, no glare, full spread.

sampleDocs.title: Sample marriage certificate (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Registered marriage proof — explain translation and notarization if needed.

sampleDocs.title: Sample relationship timeline & photos guide (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Collage layout for chat logs, travel photos, and ceremony pictures.

sampleDocs.title: Sample sponsor / undertaking letter (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Sponsor income, accommodation, and maintenance commitment.

sampleDocs.title: Sample bank statement (mock)
sampleDocs.description: 6-month statement with name, account number, and closing balance — explain seasoning.

sampleDocs.title: Sample chat / communication evidence (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Redacted WhatsApp or email excerpts — privacy-safe specimen.
